executive_summary: RCA-confirmed (conf 0.95, corroborated by session 32925831): the canonical worker /checkin loop is STRUCTURALLY BLIND to coordinator->worker push. worker-checkin.cjs (resolveCheckin) surfaces ONLY WORK_ASSIGNMENT — COACHING / advisory INFO / coordinator_reply rows targeting the worker are NEVER returned, so a worker running only /checkin never sees coordinator guidance unless it MANUALLY queries session_coordination. Secondary: callsign lives only in claude_sessions.metadata + the statusline and is never re-hydrated from the durable SET_IDENTITY row, so it goes null after a release/sweep. This SD makes /checkin surface coordinator push as a durable, non-draining coordinator_messages[] on EVERY return path, and re-hydrates the callsign from the SET_IDENTITY row on every checkin. It reuses the existing read/ack split (read_at=DELIVERED, acknowledged_at=CONSUMED) + the DIRECTIVE_KINDS allowlist already in lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs — it does NOT change the coordinator-side inbox (fleet-dashboard.cjs) and does NOT alter WORK_ASSIGNMENT handling.

functional_requirements.title: Surface coordinator->worker push in /checkin as coordinator_messages[]
functional_requirements.description: In worker-checkin.cjs resolveCheckin, after `base` is built, read the directed inbox for UNCONSUMED push targeting THIS session and attach base.coordinator_messages = [{id, message_type, kind, subject, body, created_at}]. Because every return path spreads ...base, the array surfaces on resume / idle / self_claimed / self_claimed_qf alike (a BUSY claim-holder and an IDLE worker both see coaching). Selection: target_session=self; surface while acknowledged_at IS NULL (genuinely unconsumed — survives read_at being set by the PostToolUse hook); message_type='COACHING' OR (message_type='INFO' with payload.kind in {coordinator_reply, adam_advisory} or a coordinator INFO with NO payload.signal_type and NO payload.kind=roll_call). EXCLUDE: payload.signal_type IS NOT NULL (friction channel), payload.kind='roll_call' (the worker's own availability ping), WORK_ASSIGNMENT (already surfaced as pending_work_assignment), SET_IDENTITY (handled by FR-2).
acceptance_criteria: a worker running ONLY /checkin gets coordinator_messages[] containing an unconsumed COACHING row targeting it
coordinator_messages[] surfaces on EVERY action (resume, idle, self_claimed, ...), not just one path
friction signals (payload.signal_type) and the worker's own roll_call are NOT surfaced as coordinator_messages

functional_requirements.title: Durable callsign re-hydration from the SET_IDENTITY row
functional_requirements.description: When callsign is null from claude_sessions.metadata, re-hydrate it from the most-recent durable SET_IDENTITY row (message_type='SET_IDENTITY', target_session=self, payload.callsign present) and write it back to claude_sessions.metadata.fleet_identity (service client) so it survives the NEXT release/sweep too. The re-hydrated callsign flows into base.callsign + the roll_call registration in the SAME response.
acceptance_criteria: a session whose claude_sessions.metadata lost its callsign re-hydrates it from the SET_IDENTITY row on checkin
the re-hydrated callsign appears in the same checkin response (base.callsign) and is persisted back to metadata
no SET_IDENTITY row -> callsign stays null (no fabrication), fail-open

functional_requirements.title: Non-draining delivery with bounded consumption (no lose-on-missed-render, no infinite re-surface)
functional_requirements.description: /checkin is the AUTHORITATIVE delivery channel (its JSON is the worker's decision input), replacing the unreliable ephemeral PostToolUse render as the consumption point. On surfacing a push row via coordinator_messages[]: if read_at IS NULL, stamp read_at=now (DELIVERED) but leave acknowledged_at NULL -> the row RE-SURFACES on the next checkin (guards against a single missed render). On a SUBSEQUENT checkin where read_at is already set, stamp acknowledged_at=now for NON-directive (advisory) kinds -> consumed, stops surfacing (>=2 authoritative deliveries guaranteed, then bounded). DIRECTIVE_KINDS rows are NEVER auto-acked here (read_at only) — genuine action stamps acknowledged_at, exactly as ackMessage already does. Reuse ws.DIRECTIVE_KINDS (import, never duplicate). The PostToolUse hook (coordination-inbox.cjs) already surfaces-not-drains these (read_at left NULL); this SD does not regress that.
acceptance_criteria: an unconsumed push row is re-surfaced if read_at was set but acknowledged_at was not (a single missed render does not lose it)
an advisory push row is acknowledged (stops surfacing) after >=2 authoritative checkin deliveries — never infinite
DIRECTIVE_KINDS rows are never auto-acknowledged by the surfacing path (read_at only)

functional_requirements.title: Tests (regression pins)
functional_requirements.description: tests/unit pins for: (a) coordinator_messages[] includes a seeded COACHING row for a worker running only /checkin; (b) friction (signal_type) + roll_call + WORK_ASSIGNMENT + SET_IDENTITY are excluded from coordinator_messages; (c) callsign re-hydrates from a seeded SET_IDENTITY row + persists; (d) re-surface-on-missed-render (read_at set, ack null -> re-surfaces) then bounded-ack (advisory acked after 2nd delivery); (e) DIRECTIVE_KINDS never auto-acked. Pure/seeded fixtures with an injected supabase stub (resolveCheckin already takes an injected client).
acceptance_criteria: the exclusion + re-surface + bounded-ack + directive-no-autoack behaviors are each pinned
tests use the injected-client pattern (no live DB)

technical_requirements.title: Reuse existing contracts
technical_requirements.description: Import ws.DIRECTIVE_KINDS + ws.PAYLOAD_KINDS + the SESSION_COORDINATION_COLUMNS from lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs — never duplicate the allowlist (QF-20260610-545 lesson). Add an `unackedOnly`/acknowledged_at-IS-NULL read option to getMessagesForSession (or a sibling reader) rather than hand-rolling the query in worker-checkin.

technical_requirements.title: Fail-open everywhere
technical_requirements.description: Every new read/stamp is best-effort and try/caught — a query error must preserve today's checkin behavior (never throw, never block the handshake). Mirrors the existing pending_work_assignment peek.

technical_requirements.title: Out of scope
technical_requirements.description: Do NOT change the coordinator-side inbox (fleet-dashboard.cjs printInbox stays the coordinator view). Do NOT alter WORK_ASSIGNMENT acquisition/handling. Do NOT change the friction signal router. Do NOT add a new table or column (read_at/acknowledged_at already exist).

test_scenarios.scenario: Worker running only /checkin surfaces a seeded unconsumed COACHING row in coordinator_messages[]
Friction signal (signal_type), own roll_call, WORK_ASSIGNMENT, SET_IDENTITY are excluded from coordinator_messages
test_scenarios.expected: none of these appear in coordinator_messages[]

test_scenarios.scenario: callsign re-hydrates from a seeded SET_IDENTITY row when metadata lost it
Re-surface on missed render then bounded ack
test_scenarios.expected: row re-surfaces while acknowledged_at NULL; advisory acked after 2nd checkin delivery; directive never auto-acked

acceptance_criteria: A worker running ONLY /checkin surfaces (and can act on) coordinator COACHING / advisory INFO / coordinator_reply via coordinator_messages[] on every return path — closing the structural-blindness RCA (conf 0.95)
callsign re-hydrates durably from the SET_IDENTITY row on every checkin and survives release/sweep

push rows are NOT auto-drained on delivery: a single missed render does not lose a row (re-surfaces), and advisory rows are bounded-acked after authoritative delivery (no infinite noise); DIRECTIVE_KINDS never auto-acked
coordinator-side inbox + WORK_ASSIGNMENT handling + friction router unchanged; no schema change; adversarial review of delivery/idempotency/re-hydration/ordering clean

risks.risk: Infinite re-surface (a push row never gets acked -> noise every checkin)
risks.mitigation: FR-3 bounded consumption: advisory rows ack after >=2 authoritative checkin deliveries (read_at set -> ack on next); only DIRECTIVE_KINDS persist until genuine action (by design). Pinned by TS-4.

risks.risk: Over-surfacing the worker's OWN roll_call INFO or friction signals as 'coaching'
risks.mitigation: Explicit excludes: payload.kind=roll_call, payload.signal_type IS NOT NULL, WORK_ASSIGNMENT, SET_IDENTITY. The worker's roll_call targets the COORDINATOR (target_session=coordinator) so it cannot match target_session=self anyway. Pinned by TS-2.

risks.risk: Regressing the existing read/ack split or the pending_work_assignment peek
risks.mitigation: Reuse ws.DIRECTIVE_KINDS; do not touch WORK_ASSIGNMENT path; fail-open on all new reads; the PostToolUse hook already surfaces-not-drains. Adversarial review on ordering/idempotency.

consumers: the LEO fleet worker /loop (reads coordinator_messages[] each checkin to act on coaching)
the coordinator (its push now reliably reaches busy + idle workers)
data_contracts: read session_coordination + SET_IDENTITY rows; stamp read_at/acknowledged_at per the read/ack split; persist callsign to claude_sessions.metadata; NO schema change
observability_rollout: coordinator_messages[] visible in worker-checkin.cjs JSON output; unit pins assert delivery + exclusion + re-surface + bounded-ack

system_architecture.summary: Additive changes to the worker PULL path: worker-checkin.cjs gains coordinator_messages[] (non-draining, bounded-ack) + callsign re-hydration; a small reader option in lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs (acknowledged_at IS NULL). Coordinator side + friction router + WORK_ASSIGNMENT path untouched.

components.name: scripts/worker-checkin.cjs
components.change: MODIFY: add coordinator_messages[] surfacing to base + callsign re-hydration + bounded read/ack stamping (FR-1/2/3)

components.name: lib/fleet/worker-status.cjs
components.change: MODIFY: add an acknowledged_at-IS-NULL read option (unackedOnly) + a SET_IDENTITY reader helper (FR-1/2)

implementation_approach.summary: Reuse the existing read/ack split + DIRECTIVE_KINDS allowlist; make /checkin the authoritative non-draining delivery + bounded consumption point for coordinator push, and re-hydrate the callsign durably. Adversarial-review the delivery/idempotency/ordering/re-hydration before merge.
steps: add unackedOnly + SET_IDENTITY reader to worker-status.cjs
surface coordinator_messages[] in resolveCheckin base + bounded read/ack stamping

smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: all new pins pass (coordinator_messages surfacing, exclusions, callsign re-hydration, re-surface + bounded-ack, directive-no-autoack)

smoke_test_steps.instruction: node scripts/worker-checkin.cjs with a seeded unread COACHING row targeting the session
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: the JSON output includes coordinator_messages[] containing the COACHING row; the row is not auto-stamped acknowledged_at on first delivery
